primary_sources.title: Meet Codex for Students. We're offering college students in the U.S. and Canada $100 in Codex credits. Our goal is to support students to learn by building, breaking, and fixing things. http://chatgpt.com/codex/students

Our team had a great week in San Jose at @NVIDIAGTC ! Cohere leaders and technical staff delivered talks across the event, covering hardware efficiency, enterprise-grade automation, and innovation across the stack. Our CEO, @aidangomez, also joined the GTC live stage to discuss the exciting possibilities for enterprise customers, the barriers to deploying enterprise technology into global economies, and what surprised him most about the evolution of AI adoption. It was great connecting with our customers and partners, including @Dell, @Microsoft, @Oracle, and @CoreWeave, as well as the many other brilliant minds across the ecosystem who are shaping the exciting future of AI. #NVIDIAGTC

supporting_sources.title: Two weeks. Seven briefs. Up to $100K in cash prizes to make any ad you can imagine. Bring your biggest, most ambitious ideas to life with Runway. No client notes required. Runway's Big Ad Contest For Products That Don't Exist. Learn more at the link below.

Most people have no idea this is happening: Your AI Agent can now have a job on its own! Check out the open-source project I'm linking below. The project is an AI agent skill that lets any compatible agent (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Gemini CLI, etc.) interact with the AWP (Agent Working Protocol). You can install and use this skill *right now*. You can have your agents register on the network, find available work, and start completing it to earn money. Registration is free.
Controlling Claude Code from my phone is next-level coding p0-rn. I don't want to hear your "oPeNcLaW dOeS tHat" crap. I don't care.
